column2:
Acting without worrying about or being bothered by something (often negative).
Connection: Nも / Vるのも + かまわず
Ex: 彼女は人目もかまわず子どものように泣いた。 — She cried like a child without caring about people watching.
彼は靴が脱げるのもかまわず走り続けた。 — He kept running without caring that his shoes came off.

Doing something regardless of an obstacle or unexpected situation.
Connection: V/Aにも / na/N + にもかかわらず
Ex: 雨にもかかわらず、大勢の人々が集まった。 — Despite the rain, many people gathered.
見たにもかかわらず、彼は見なかったと言った。 — Despite having seen it, he said he didn't.

Doing something regardless of conditions (e.g., whether X or Y, age, nationality).
Connection: Vる-Vないに / Aい-Aくないに / N + かかわらず
Ex: 来る来ないにかかわらず、連絡をください。 — Please contact me regardless of whether you are coming or not.
国籍にかかわらず、歓迎します。 — We welcome everyone regardless of nationality.

A condition (age, gender, etc.) does not matter for the action.
Connection: N + を問わず
Ex: 年齢を問わず多くの人々が集まった。 — Many people of all ages gathered.
経験の有無を問わず、募集。 — Recruiting regardless of experience.

column1: 〜やら〜やら (and... and... / such things as...)
column2:
Listing examples (often overwhelming or chaotic).
Connection: N/A/V + やら〜やら
Ex: 机の上は本やらノートやらでいっぱいだ。 — The desk is covered with books, notebooks, and so on.
この季節は、目がかゆいやら鼻水が出るやら、大変です。 — This season is hard because of things like itchy eyes and a runny nose.

column1: 〜につけ〜につけ (whether X or Y / whenever X or Y)
column2:
Whether doing X or Y; in every case of X or Y.
Connection: Vる/Aい + につけ〜につけ
Ex: 写真を見るにつけ、歌を聞くにつけ、国を思い出す。 — Whenever I look at photos or listen to songs, I remember my country.
いいにつけ悪いにつけ、子は親に似る。 — Whether good or bad, children resemble their parents.

column1: 〜にしろ〜にしろ (whether X or Y / even if X or Y)
column2:
Regardless of the options; both cases have the same outcome.
Connection: V/A/na/N + にしろ〜にしろ
Ex: 行くにしろ行かないにしろ、連絡してください。 — Regardless of whether you go or not, please contact me.
受験はしないにしろ、勉強はしなさい。 — Even if you don't take the entrance exam, you should study.

column1: 〜も〜ば〜も (X is X, and Y is Y)
column2:
Emphasizing that both X and Y share a quality.
Connection: N1もVばN2もVる / N1もAければN2もAい
Ex: 彼は勉強もできればスポーツもできる。 — He is good at studying and also good at sports.
私は歌も下手ならダンスも下手だ。 — I am bad at singing and dancing.

column1: 〜だけましだ (better than nothing / at least)
column2:
Although not perfect, it's at least something to be grateful for.
Connection: V/A/na + だけましだ
Ex: 君は仕事があるだけましだよ。俺は首になってしまった。 — You should be grateful for even having a job. I was fired.
給料が減ったけれど、首にならないだけましだ。 — Although my pay decreased, at least I didn't get fired.

Practically the same as something else.
Connection: N + (も)同然だ
Ex: この車は中古車といっても新品(も)同然だ。 — Although this is a used car, it is virtually new.
彼の財産はないも同然だ。 — He has virtually no assets.

column1: 〜(も)当然だ / 当たり前だ (it is natural)
column2:
It is expected or natural.
Connection: V/A/na/N + 当然だ / 当たり前だ
Ex: ひどいことばかり言ったので、彼女に嫌われて当然だ。 — Since you said nasty things, it's natural she hates you.
相手のチームは弱い。勝って当たり前だ。 — The other team is not good, so winning is expected.

Because of X, Y happens even more so (expected consequence).
Connection: V/A/na/N + だけに
Ex: 一流ホテルだけあって快適だった。 — As expected of a first-rate hotel, it was comfortable.
このバッグは安いだけにすぐに壊れてしまった。 — Just as one would expect from a cheap bag, it broke immediately.

column1: 〜ばかりか / 〜ばかりでなく (not only X, but also Y)
column2:
Emphasizing an additional fact (positive or negative).
Connection: V/A/na/N + ばかりか
Ex: あの人は学校の成績がいいばかりかスポーツもできる。 — He not only has good grades, but can also do sports.
このあたりは、空気ばかりでなく水も汚染されている。 — In this area, not only is the air polluted, but so is the water.

A negative outcome happened specifically because of reason X.
Connection: V/A/na/N + ばかりに
Ex: あの飛行機に乗ったばかりに、彼は死んだ。 — Just because he took that plane, he died.
英語の先生が嫌いなばかりに、英語も嫌いになってしまった。 — Just because I disliked the English teacher, I came to dislike English.

Strong suggestion or resolution.
Connection: Vよう + ではないか
Ex: 今日はお祝いだ。みんなで飲もうではないか。 — It's a celebration today. Let's all drink!
だれもやらないなら、ぼくがやってみようじゃないか。 — If no one else does it, I'll do it!

There is no method or means to perform an action.
Connection: Vます + ようがない
Ex: 何と言ったらいいのか、言いようがない。 — I really don't know how to express it.
この作文は日本語がめちゃくちゃで直しようがない。 — This essay's Japanese is such a mess, there is no way to fix it.

Expresses something that appears or behaves like something else.
Connection: V/A/na/N + かのようだ
Ex: まるで空が泣いているかのようだ。 — It is as if the sky is crying.
怖いものでも見たかのように、彼女は震えていた。 — She was shivering as if she had seen something frightening.

Indicates low probability of an event happening.
Connection: Vます + そうにない
Ex: 最近仕事がすごく忙しくて、同窓会に行けそうにないよ。 — I'm so busy at work, I don't think I can go to the reunion.

Used when starting something official/important; formal occasion.
Connection: Vる/N + にあたり
Ex: 図書館の利用にあたり、図書カードが必要です。 — Upon using the library, a library card is necessary.

Following a plan, rule, or experience.
Connection: N + に基づいて
Ex: この工事は市の計画に基づいて進められます。 — This construction is proceeding based on the city's plan.

column1: 〜に応じて (in response to / depending on)
column2:
Responding to needs or conditions.
Connection: N + に応じて
Ex: ソフトは、必要に応じてダウンロードしてください。 — Please download the software as necessary.

Under the influence or guidance of X.
Connection: N + の下で
Ex: 子どもたちが青空の下で元気に遊んでいる。 — Children are playing cheerfully under the blue sky.
